source: Initial web claim boundary for the master-plan pipeline transition
claims_allowed: Compact scene and dataset inventory summaries backed by checked-in derived JSON
Document-grouping summaries for implemented methods when they are backed by named derived groupings artifacts

Quantization summaries for implemented configs when the claim is limited to vocabulary size, document length, bin population, reconstruction error, entropy proxies, or quantizer-level sensitivity present in data/derived/quantization/
Recipe summaries for named corpora when they are backed by data/derived/recipes/ artifacts and the UI states the recipe, document constructor, quantization config, and any sampling caveat
Corpus preview definitions that explicitly state alphabet, word, document, corpus, and caveats
Compact local-core benchmark summaries for labeled, unlabeled, unmixing, and measured-target cases
Compact HIDSAG subset inventory, region-document, band-quality, and preprocessing-sensitivity summaries
Segmentation baseline summaries and preview overlays generated offline
Derived PCA or KMeans analytical diagnostics computed from compact derived assets
claims_blocked: Any topic-view, downstream-LDA quantization claim, or cross-method claim not present in derived/manifests/index.json
Any measurement or label association claim lacking a named derived artifact
Any on-the-fly computation in the web layer from raw or local-only assets
notes: This contract is intentionally conservative while the current derived tree is still transitional.
Wordification claims must expose whether the corpus is full-inventory or sampled, especially for pixel-document slices.
The claim boundary should widen only when dedicated master-plan builders land and publish named artifacts.
